title: Charles Barkley impressed by Jayson Tatum in return, but doesn’t view Celtics as favorites in East
author: Conor Roche
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned from an Achilles tear, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in the Celtics' win.
Charles Barkley was impressed with Tatum but still favors the Cavaliers to win the Eastern Conference after their recent success.
The Celtics have matched the Spurs' 22 wins since January 1, despite Tatum's absence for most of that period.

title: Mass. man accused of impersonating an Army veteran for more than 30 years
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: James D. Sommers of Pittsfield charged with posing as an Army veteran since the 1990s for benefits.
Sommers allegedly used a stolen identity to receive nearly $30,000 in VA medical care and Social Security.
Sommers faces up to five years in prison for false statements, with additional fines if convicted.

title: Watch: Body cam video shows officer rescuing man, 78, from burning truck
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Easton Police and two bystanders rescued a 78-year-old man from a burning truck after a crash on Route 138.
The crash involved three vehicles; a Mack dump truck, a Ford F-350, and a Toyota Camry that caught fire.
Francis Leverone was hospitalized with serious injuries, and the crash is under ongoing investigation by Easton police.

title: US attorney going after money forfeited by Mass. resident in romance crypto fraud scheme
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Federal prosecutors are seeking to recover $327,000 in cryptocurrency from a Massachusetts resident linked to a fraud scheme.
The scheme involved a fake online relationship where the victim was convinced to invest in cryptocurrency, later deemed fraudulent.
Authorities seized cryptocurrency wallets in August 2025, arguing they contained funds laundered through various digital wallets.

title: Jayson Tatum’s fashion also makes a return in his comeback from Achilles tear
author: LaDonna LaGuerre
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Boston Celtics lineup, achieving a double-double in 27 minutes after recovering from an Achilles tear.
Tatum's pregame fashion included Parisian luxury and '90s nostalgia, featuring Lanvin denim and Timberland boots.
He wore the 'Bruce Lee' Jordan Tatum 4s, symbolizing his disciplined basketball approach, alongside a stylish, simple wardrobe.

title: She survived a brain tumor. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Tracy Haisley, a brain tumor survivor, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her recovery journey.
Haisley, from Columbia, SC, underwent brain surgery and three months of rehab before aiming for the marathon.
She serves on the Richland Library Board and joined the Boston Public Library team, highlighting her passion for public libraries.

title: What Jaylen Brown said of Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Celtics after a 10-month recovery from a ruptured Achilles, scoring 15 points against the Mavericks.
Jaylen Brown expressed appreciation for Tatum's return, highlighting his commitment to the team amid their title aspirations.
Brown and Tatum plan to collaborate closely, focusing on maintaining the Celtics' winning momentum as Tatum reacclimates.

title: Cooper Flagg was asked if he wants to be a Celtic one day. Here’s what the Maine native said.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Cooper Flagg, the Mavericks' No. 1 pick, expressed satisfaction with his team despite his Maine roots.
Flagg remarked on the thrill of playing at TD Garden, a venue he frequented as a child.
Flagg is contracted with Dallas until 2030, with a club option for 2028 and 2029.

title: With Jayson Tatum back in the fold, these Celtics know their story is just beginning
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned for the Celtics after 42 weeks, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and seven assists in their win.
Despite a slow start, Tatum showed resilience, gradually finding his rhythm against the Dallas Mavericks in a 25-point Celtics victory.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's effort and mindset in his return, noting smooth integration with the updated roster.

title: Jayson Tatum’s return was essentially perfect: 7 takeaways as Celtics blow out Mavericks
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned from a torn Achilles and started for the Celtics in their victory over the Mavericks.
Tatum played without a minutes restriction, contributing 15 points, 12 rebounds, and 7 assists in a 120-100 win.
Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 24 points, highlighting a balanced performance with six players reaching double figures.
